items.curatedReasonEn: WHO's emphasis on investing in "people" rather than just "devices" in rehabilitation strongly aligns with Cadence's advocacy for person-centered, profession-led rehabilitation. This is a guiding call to action for the global physical therapy profession.

items.title: Trends in Intrinsic Capacity: Evidence from 32 Countries
items.summary: This study investigates changes and temporal trends in intrinsic capacity across a broad range of 32 countries to better understand its current state in various population groups, aiming to promote healthy aging.
items.curatedReason: 内源性能力（Intrinsic Capacity）是健康老龄化的核心指标。这项涵盖32个国家的研究，能让你了解全球人口在内源性能力方面的变化趋势，为制定老年康复策略提供宏观参考。
items.titleZh: 内源性能力趋势：来自32个国家的证据
items.summaryZh: 这项研究调查了32个国家内源性能力的变化和时间趋势，旨在更好地了解其在不同人群中的现状，以促进健康老龄化。
items.curatedReasonEn: Intrinsic Capacity is a key indicator of healthy aging. This study, covering 32 countries, provides insights into global trends in intrinsic capacity, offering a macro-level reference for developing geriatric rehabilitation strategies.
items.limitation: 研究设计未明确，无法评估因果关系和具体干预效果。
items.limitationEn: The study design is not specified, preventing evaluation of causality and specific intervention effects.
items.journal: Archives of Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation

items.title: Sociodemographic Determinants of Food Insecurity and Its Association with BMI in Individuals with Spinal Cord Injury: A Cross-Sectional Analysis from the All of Us Research Program
items.summary: A cross-sectional analysis using the All of Us Research Program investigates sociodemographic determinants of food insecurity and its association with BMI in individuals with spinal cord injury compared to a control cohort without disability.
items.curatedReason: 这项研究揭示了脊髓损伤（SCI）人群食物不安全状况的社会经济决定因素及其与BMI的关联。物理治疗师在全面照护SCI患者时，需要关注这些非临床因素，必要时转诊社会服务。
items.titleZh: 脊髓损伤个体食物不安全的社会人口学决定因素及其与BMI的关联：来自“All of Us”研究项目的横断面分析
items.summaryZh: 一项利用“All of Us”研究项目的横断面分析，调查了脊髓损伤个体食物不安全的社会人口学决定因素及其与BMI的关联，并与无残疾对照组进行了比较。
items.curatedReasonEn: This study reveals the sociodemographic determinants of food insecurity and its association with BMI in individuals with spinal cord injury (SCI). Physical therapists, in providing holistic care for SCI patients, need to be aware of these non-clinical factors and refer to social services when necessary.
items.limitation: 横断面研究设计无法确定食物不安全与BMI之间的因果关系。
items.limitationEn: The cross-sectional study design prevents determining causality between food insecurity and BMI.
items.journal: Archives of Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation

items.title: Correlates of Loneliness in Persons with Spinal Cord Injury
items.summary: This study explores the correlates of loneliness in persons with spinal cord injury, addressing a global public health problem exacerbated by debilitating injuries and increasing use of isolating technologies.
items.curatedReason: 孤独感是影响脊髓损伤（SCI）患者康复结局的重要心理社会因素。这项研究有助于你更好地理解SCI患者的孤独感相关因素，从而在康复计划中纳入社会心理支持策略。
items.titleZh: 脊髓损伤患者孤独感的关联因素
items.summaryZh: 这项研究探讨了脊髓损伤患者孤独感的关联因素，孤独感是全球公共卫生问题，在致残性损伤和日益隔离的技术使用下尤为突出。
items.curatedReasonEn: Loneliness is a significant psychosocial factor affecting rehabilitation outcomes in individuals with spinal cord injury (SCI). This study helps you better understand the correlates of loneliness in SCI patients, enabling the integration of psychosocial support strategies into rehabilitation plans.
items.limitation: 研究设计未明确，无法评估干预措施对孤独感的影响。
items.limitationEn: The study design is not specified, preventing evaluation of intervention effects on loneliness.
items.journal: Archives of Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation

items.title: Time to monitor the mental health of esports athletes
items.summary: An editorial calls for monitoring the mental health of esports athletes following the IOC's recognition of the Olympic Esports Games, urging sports medicine and public health stakeholders to address this as a legitimate high-performance context.
items.curatedReason: 随着电子竞技被国际奥委会认可，电竞选手心理健康成为新的关注点。物理治疗师在预防和管理运动损伤方面有经验，应思考如何将专业技能拓展到这一新兴领域。
items.titleZh: 是时候关注电竞运动员的心理健康了
items.summaryZh: 一篇社论呼吁在国际奥委会认可奥林匹克电竞运动后，应关注电竞运动员的心理健康，敦促运动医学和公共卫生利益相关者将其视为一个合法的高绩效背景来处理。
items.curatedReasonEn: With esports gaining IOC recognition, the mental health of esports athletes is a growing concern. Physical therapists, with their experience in injury prevention and management, should consider how to extend their expertise to this emerging field.

items.title: Period pain in female athletes: a call to action - time to fill the evidence gap and develop tailored management pathways
items.summary: An editorial highlights the high prevalence of period pain (dysmenorrhea) in female athletes (60%) and its negative impact on sports performance, calling for more evidence and tailored management pathways.
items.curatedReason: 痛经在女运动员中普遍存在，且严重影响运动表现。作为临床物理治疗师，我们需要认识到这一问题，并与跨学科团队协作，为女运动员提供量身定制的康复和管理路径，这涉及到骨科（盆底）或运动康复范畴。
items.titleZh: 女运动员痛经：一项行动呼吁——是时候填补证据空白并开发定制化管理路径了
items.summaryZh: 一篇社论强调了女运动员（60%）痛经的高发病率及其对运动表现的负面影响，呼吁开展更多研究并开发定制化的管理路径。

items.title: Planting the seeds of power: paediatric powerpenia as a lifecourse research priority
items.summary: An editorial introduces "paediatric powerpenia" as a lifecourse research priority, advocating for a shift in perspective towards preventing low muscular power in children due to its lifelong implications for musculoskeletal health.
items.curatedReason: “儿童期肌力缺乏”（paediatric powerpenia）是新提出的概念，强调儿童期肌肉力量不足对终生骨骼肌肉健康的影响。这提示物理治疗师需要更早地关注儿童的肌肉力量训练，将预防理念融入早期干预和儿童康复中。
items.titleZh: 播种力量的种子：将儿童期肌力缺乏作为生命历程研究的优先事项
items.summaryZh: 一篇社论将“儿童期肌力缺乏”引入为生命历程研究的优先事项，倡导将视角转向预防儿童肌肉力量不足，因其对肌肉骨骼健康具有终生影响。
items.curatedReasonEn: “Paediatric powerpenia” is a newly proposed concept highlighting the lifelong impact of insufficient muscle power in childhood on musculoskeletal health. This suggests that physical therapists need to focus on muscle power training in children earlier, integrating preventive concepts into early intervention and pediatric rehabilitation.

items.title: Rehabilitation and outcomes following revision total hip replacement: patients experiences, optimised rehabilitation interventions and opioid use (PhD Academy Award)
items.summary: A PhD Academy Award summarizes research on rehabilitation and opioid use after revision total hip replacement, examining patient experiences, the effectiveness of targeted exercise programs, and predictors of persistent opioid use over two years.
items.curatedReason: 这项博士研究深入探讨了髋关节置换术后康复、阿片类药物使用和患者体验，涵盖了功能表现、定制化运动方案以及阿片类药物依赖的风险因素。这对临床制定髋关节置换术后康复策略具有重要参考价值。
items.titleZh: 翻修全髋关节置换术后的康复和结局：患者体验、优化康复干预和阿片类药物使用（博士学院奖）
items.summaryZh: 一项博士学院奖研究总结了翻修全髋关节置换术后康复和阿片类药物使用的研究，探讨了患者体验、靶向运动方案的有效性以及两年内持续阿片类药物使用的预测因素。
items.curatedReasonEn: This PhD research deeply explores rehabilitation, opioid use, and patient experiences following revision total hip replacement, covering functional performance, tailored exercise programs, and risk factors for opioid dependence. This provides significant reference value for developing post-revision THR rehabilitation strategies in clinical practice.
